task_completion.description: Navigate to the Add Concept Reference Term page in OpenMRS
task_completion.visual_evidence: The web browser shows the OpenMRS Administration interface, specifically the 'Add Concept Reference Term' or 'Manage Concept Reference Terms' page.
The dropdown or input field for 'Concept Source' has 'ICD-10-WHO' selected or entered.

task_completion.description: Enter the correct Code and Name
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Code' input field contains 'A00' and the 'Name' input field contains 'Cholera'.
The 'Description' text area contains the text 'Acute diarrhoeal infection caused by ingestion of food or water contaminated with the bacterium Vibrio cholerae'.

task_completion.description: Save the new Concept Reference Term successfully
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success message (e.g., 'Concept Reference Term saved successfully') is visible, or the newly created term 'A00' / 'Cholera' is shown in the list of saved Concept Reference Terms.

integrity.description: The agent used the web browser GUI to complete the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with a web browser (e.g., Firefox, Chrome) rather than terminal commands like curl or direct database SQL inserts.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the correct OpenMRS instance
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser address bar shows a URL starting with 'https://localhost/openmrs/admin'.

integrity.description: The agent did not use external automation scripts to bypass manual interaction
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal windows open showing the execution of Python/Selenium or bash scripts designed to automate the web interactions.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Verify the following exact values in the OpenMRS form: Concept Source must be 'ICD-10-WHO'. Code must be 'A00'. Name must be 'Cholera'. The Description must contain the keywords 'acute', 'diarrhoeal', 'vibrio', and 'cholerae'. The correct application URL is 'https://localhost/openmrs/admin'.
